title: Curved Flow, Sticky Stack, Char Reveal, Gravity Trail, Spring Sheet
description: Five live UI pattern demos: Bézier thumbnail flow, CSS sticky deck, per-character lift reveal, gravity particle trail, spring snap bottom sheet.

drops.title: Curved-Path Thumbnail Flow
drops.description: Stacked photo cards expand along a configurable Bézier arc with spring interpolation in 4 path modes.
drops.concept: Recreates the MotionPath thumbnail flow technique from Codrops (June 2026). Six cards stack with rotational offset, then spring-animate to positions distributed along a cubic Bézier curve. Modes include Arc, Fan, Wave, and Spiral. Pure vanilla JS — no GSAP dependency.
drops.source: Codrops — Creating a Thumbnail Flow Animation with GSAP MotionPath (Jun 4 2026)

interaction.click: Click mode buttons to change path shape

drops.title: Sticky Card Stack
drops.description: Six gradient cards with position:sticky at progressive offsets layer into a physical deck as you scroll — zero JavaScript for the core effect.
drops.concept: The classic 'sticky stack' layout pattern achieves a physical deck-of-cards feel using only CSS. Each card's top value is 20px + (index × 32px), so they accumulate at the viewport top as the user scrolls past them. The technique needs zero JavaScript for the stacking behavior — only the debug overlay uses JS.
drops.source: CSS position:sticky stacking — widely used in modern editorial and product pages

drops.title: Character Lift Reveal
drops.description: Headlines where every character slides up from a hidden clip zone on scroll entry, with staggered delays per character.
drops.concept: Each character of a reveal headline is wrapped in an overflow:hidden container. On IntersectionObserver entry, a CSS class fires transitions that move all characters from translateY(105%) to 0, with incremental delay (38ms/char or 22ms/char in fast mode). Works across multiple font sizes and 4 themed sections with a replay button.
drops.source: Per-character reveal technique widely seen in editorial and portfolio sites (2025-2026 trend)

drops.title: Gravity Particle Trail
drops.description: Canvas particles spawn at the cursor, fall under adjustable gravity, and bounce off the floor — click for explosion bursts.
drops.concept: A Float32Array particle pool (max 2000) spawns particles at mouse/touch position with random upward velocity. Each frame applies configurable gravity and dampened floor/wall bouncing. Four color palettes (Fire, Ocean, Galaxy, Neon) and three tunable sliders (gravity, spawn rate, bounce). Inspired by Codrops 'Gravity-Based Mouse Trail' (May 2026).
drops.source: Codrops — Made With GSAP: Building a Fun Gravity-Based Mouse Trail (May 20, 2026)

drops.title: Spring Snap Bottom Sheet
drops.description: Mobile bottom drawer snapping to 3 height positions (12%/55%/90%) via spring physics — fling velocity biases the target snap.
drops.concept: A full-featured bottom sheet implementing spring physics with configurable stiffness (220) and damping (28). The sheet tracks pointer drag velocity; on release, velocity biases which snap point is selected, enabling natural fling gestures. Three snap states: Peek (12vh), Half (55vh), Full (90vh). Works identically with mouse and touch via Pointer Events API.
drops.source: Mobile bottom sheet UX pattern (iOS/Android) — reimplemented with spring physics
